notes: Wave 1 — Infrastructure layer. All 9 WOs must merge before Wave 2 starts. Batch 1 is fully independent. Batch 2 waits for Batch 1.

batches.name: Fully Independent — Fire Immediately
batches.rationale: These 5 WOs touch completely different files and have no inter-dependencies. All depend only on the Wave 0 foundation which is on main.

tasks.title: Identity Service: Auth (register, login, logout, JWT, sessions)
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-001-identity-auth.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are implementing the auth module for the identity-service. Create the branch wave1/identity-auth from main.

Files to create (listed in the WO):
- services/identity-service/src/auth/auth.handler.ts
- services/identity-service/src/auth/auth.service.ts
- services/identity-service/src/auth/auth.repository.ts
- services/identity-service/src/index.ts (copy from services/_template, update name/port)
- services/identity-service/src/routes.ts
- services/identity-service/package.json
- services/identity-service/tsconfig.json
- services/identity-service/.env.example
- tests/unit/auth.service.test.ts
- tests/integration/auth.handler.test.ts

IMPORTANT:
- Import all types from @clickup/contracts (read-only)
- Import all middleware/utils from @clickup/sdk (read-only)
- NEVER use console.log — use logger from @clickup/sdk
- NEVER throw raw Error — use AppError(ErrorCode.X)
- NEVER write validation logic — use validate(Schema, data) from @clickup/sdk
- The users table does NOT have password_hash yet — run this migration first in identity-service/migrations/001_add_password_hash.sql: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS password_hash TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
- Start from services/_template for index.ts and package.json
- PORT=3001

Read AGENTS.md for full architecture rules. Read the WO for mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-001): identity auth — register, login, logout, JWT, sessions'

tasks.title: API Gateway: Routing & Proxy
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-005-gateway-routing.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are implementing the API gateway routing layer. Create the branch wave1/gateway-routing from main.

Files to create (listed in the WO):
- services/api-gateway/src/index.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/routes.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/proxy/proxy.config.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/proxy/proxy.middleware.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/middleware/auth-forward.ts
- services/api-gateway/package.json
- services/api-gateway/tsconfig.json
- services/api-gateway/.env.example
- tests/integration/gateway.handler.test.ts

Install these packages in api-gateway: http-proxy-middleware express-rate-limit

IMPORTANT:
- This service has NO database connection
- Use SERVICE_ROUTES array from proxy.config.ts exactly as specified in the WO
- Auth forwarding calls identity-service GET /api/v1/auth/verify
- Rate limiting uses Redis from @clickup/sdk
- x-trace-id correlation ID must propagate to all upstream requests
- PORT=3000

Read AGENTS.md for full architecture rules. Read the WO for mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-005): api-gateway — routing, proxy, auth forwarding, rate limiting'

tasks.title: DB Seed Script
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-007-db-seed.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are creating the development seed script. Create the branch wave1/db-seed from main.

Files to create:
- infra/seeds/seed.ts — main seed script (run with: pnpm seed)
- infra/seeds/fixtures/users.fixture.ts
- infra/seeds/fixtures/workspaces.fixture.ts
- infra/seeds/fixtures/tasks.fixture.ts

Add to root package.json scripts: "seed": "tsx infra/seeds/seed.ts"

The seed creates:
- 1 demo user (demo@clickup.oss / password123) with specific UUID 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
- 1 demo workspace (slug: demo) with UUID 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000010
- 2 spaces: Engineering, Marketing
- 3 lists per space
- 5 default statuses per list (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Done, Cancelled)
- 10 tasks per list
- 3 subtasks per task
- 2 comments per task

IMPORTANT:
- Seed must be idempotent (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for known UUIDs)
- Use bcrypt.hash('password123', 12) for demo user password
- Tasks need materialized path AND seq_id (see WO for exact pattern)
- The createTestTask function in the WO section 4 shows the seq_id insert pattern

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-007): db seed script — demo data and test fixtures'

tasks.title: Pact Broker Setup & Contract Validator
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-008-pact-broker.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are setting up Pact contract testing infrastructure. Create the branch wave1/pact-broker from main.

Files to create:
- infra/pact/docker-compose.pact.yml — Pact Broker + postgres sidecar
- infra/pact/README.md — how to run locally
- packages/test-helpers/src/contract-validator.ts — validateResponse() and validatePaginatedResponse() helpers
- .github/workflows/pact.yml — CI workflow for consumer pact publish + provider verification

IMPORTANT:
- The Pact Broker runs at http://localhost:9292 with credentials pact/pact
- contract-validator.ts validates response shapes against expected entity schemas using Zod
- validateResponse('task', data) returns true if valid, throws with field-level error if invalid
- The pact.yml CI workflow runs AFTER the main ci.yml
- Do NOT create packages/test-helpers/package.json (that's WO-009's job)
- Only create the contract-validator.ts file inside packages/test-helpers/src/

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-008): pact broker setup, contract validator, CI workflow'

tasks.title: Test Helpers Package
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-009-test-helpers.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are creating the shared test helpers package used by ALL service integration tests. Create the branch wave1/test-helpers from main.

Files to create:
- packages/test-helpers/package.json
- packages/test-helpers/tsconfig.json
- packages/test-helpers/src/index.ts (barrel export)
- packages/test-helpers/src/db.ts (getTestDb, closeTestDb, withRollback, setupTestDb)
- packages/test-helpers/src/auth.ts (makeTestToken, authHeader, testAuth)
- packages/test-helpers/src/request.ts (createTestRequest with AuthedRequest)
- packages/test-helpers/src/contract-validator.ts (validateResponse, validatePaginatedResponse)
- packages/test-helpers/src/fixtures/users.fixture.ts (createTestUser)
- packages/test-helpers/src/fixtures/workspaces.fixture.ts (createTestWorkspace, createTestSpace, createTestList)
- packages/test-helpers/src/fixtures/tasks.fixture.ts (createTestTask, createTestComment)

IMPORTANT:
- This package is NEVER a runtime dependency — devDependency only in services
- bcrypt cost factor 4 in fixtures for test speed (NOT production code)
- createTestList seeds exactly 3 statuses (Todo, In Progress, Done)
- createTestTask: inserts task AND task_sequences row with MAX(seq_id)+1, then updates tasks.seq_id
- withRollback always rolls back the transaction, even if the inner function throws
- makeTestToken uses signToken from @clickup/sdk (same secret as services)
- Register this package in pnpm-workspace.yaml under packages/
- Add to tsconfig.base.json references

Dependencies to add: pg, bcrypt, supertest (runtime); @pact-foundation/pact, @types/bcrypt, @types/pg, @types/supertest, typescript, zod (dev)

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-009): test-helpers package — DB fixtures, auth factory, request helper'

batches.name: Depends on Batch 1 — Fire After All Batch 1 PRs Merge
batches.rationale: WO-002/003/004 depend on WO-001 (identity auth must exist). WO-006 depends on WO-005 (gateway routing must exist). These 4 touch different files and can run in parallel within this batch.

tasks.title: Identity Service: User Profiles
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-002-identity-users.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are adding user profile management to the identity-service. Create the branch wave1/identity-users from main (after WO-001 has merged).

Files to create:
- services/identity-service/src/users/users.handler.ts
- services/identity-service/src/users/users.service.ts
- services/identity-service/src/users/users.repository.ts
- tests/unit/users.service.test.ts
- tests/integration/users.handler.test.ts

Update services/identity-service/src/routes.ts to register user routes.

IMPORTANT:
- NEVER expose password_hash in responses — use toUserDto() helper that strips it
- Batch GET /api/v1/users/batch?ids=... uses DataLoader from @clickup/sdk
- Avatar upload delegates to file-service (HTTP call, don't implement file handling here)
- Cache user profiles in Tier 2 (60s) via CacheKeys.userProfile(userId)
- Invalidate cache on profile update and password change
- Password change: verify current password with bcrypt.compare before hashing new one

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-002): identity — user profile CRUD, avatar, password change'

tasks.title: Identity Service: Workspaces & Members
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-003-identity-workspaces.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are adding workspace management to the identity-service. Create the branch wave1/identity-workspaces from main (after WO-001 has merged).

Files to create:
- services/identity-service/src/workspaces/workspaces.handler.ts
- services/identity-service/src/workspaces/workspaces.service.ts
- services/identity-service/src/workspaces/workspaces.repository.ts
- tests/unit/workspaces.service.test.ts
- tests/integration/workspaces.handler.test.ts

Update services/identity-service/src/routes.ts to register workspace routes.

IMPORTANT:
- Workspace creation is ATOMIC: workspace + owner member in one DB transaction
- Slug uniqueness check before creation — throw WORKSPACE_SLUG_TAKEN if taken
- Cannot remove the workspace owner — throw WORKSPACE_OWNER_CANNOT_LEAVE
- Invite member: user must already have an account (find by email); throw USER_NOT_FOUND if not
- Cache workspace members in Tier 2 — CacheKeys.workspaceMembers(workspaceId)
- Invalidate members cache on every member add/remove/role-change
- Publish events AFTER DB commit, NEVER inside transaction:
  - workspace.member_added on invite
  - workspace.member_removed on removal
- GET /workspaces/:id/members/:userId is called by other services for auth checks — must be fast

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-003): identity — workspace CRUD, member invite/remove/role'

tasks.title: Identity Service: Spaces & Lists
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-004-identity-spaces-lists.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are adding Spaces and Lists to the identity-service. Create the branch wave1/identity-spaces-lists from main (after WO-001 and WO-003 have merged).

Files to create:
- services/identity-service/src/spaces/spaces.handler.ts
- services/identity-service/src/spaces/spaces.service.ts
- services/identity-service/src/spaces/spaces.repository.ts
- services/identity-service/src/lists/lists.handler.ts
- services/identity-service/src/lists/lists.service.ts
- services/identity-service/src/lists/lists.repository.ts
- tests/unit/spaces.service.test.ts
- tests/unit/lists.service.test.ts
- tests/integration/spaces.handler.test.ts
- tests/integration/lists.handler.test.ts

Update services/identity-service/src/routes.ts to register space and list routes.

IMPORTANT:
- CRITICAL: GET /api/v1/lists/:id must return workspaceId (joined from spaces table) — task-service depends on this
- On list creation: seed 5 default statuses (Backlog, Todo, In Progress, Done, Cancelled) with correct status_group values from the WO
- Private spaces: only visible to creator (is_private=true → filter WHERE created_by=$userId OR is_private=FALSE)
- Soft-delete a space → also soft-delete all its lists (UPDATE lists SET deleted_at=NOW() WHERE space_id=$1)
- Use float position for ordering spaces and lists (default 65535, prepend=-10000, append=+10000)
- This query pattern for GET /lists/:id:
  SELECT l.*, s.workspace_id FROM lists l JOIN spaces s ON s.id = l.space_id WHERE l.id=$1 AND l.deleted_at IS NULL AND s.deleted_at IS NULL

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-004): identity — spaces CRUD, lists CRUD, default status seeding'

tasks.title: API Gateway: WebSocket Hub
tasks.prompt:
Read the work order at .omc/work-orders/WO-006-gateway-websocket.md — follow it EXACTLY.

You are adding WebSocket support to the API gateway. Create the branch wave1/gateway-websocket from main (after WO-005 has merged).

Files to create:
- services/api-gateway/src/websocket/ws.server.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/websocket/ws.auth.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/websocket/ws.rooms.ts
- services/api-gateway/src/websocket/ws.nats-bridge.ts
- tests/integration/websocket.test.ts

Add to api-gateway: pnpm add ws && pnpm add -D @types/ws

IMPORTANT:
- Use native ws package — NOT socket.io
- Clients connect to ws://gateway:3000/ws — path is /ws
- JWT token from query param ?token= OR Authorization header
- On connect: auto-join workspace:{workspaceId} and user:{userId} rooms
- Client can join list:{id} and task:{id} rooms on demand — validate prefix before joining
- NATS bridge: subscribe to ALL_EVENTS from @clickup/contracts, use EmitRules to determine target rooms
- Message deduplication: client in multiple target rooms receives event only ONCE (use seen Set)
- Heartbeat: ping every 30s, terminate if no pong within interval
- WS close code 4001 = Unauthorized (no/invalid token)

Do NOT store WS state in Redis — single instance for now.

Read AGENTS.md. Read WO mandatory tests — all must pass.

Create a PR titled 'feat(WO-006): api-gateway — WebSocket hub, rooms, NATS bridge, heartbeat'
